description:
You are acting as a capacity-planner who wants to know the single highest (peak) CPU-usage value that each server ever reached during a three-day measurement period.

The raw measurements are already present in:
  /home/user/data/usage.log

Content of /home/user/data/usage.log (9 lines, tab-separated by exactly one <space> between the columns):

serverA 2023-07-01 24
serverB 2023-07-01 67
serverC 2023-07-01 53
serverA 2023-07-02 21
serverB 2023-07-02 79
serverC 2023-07-02 48
serverA 2023-07-03 35
serverB 2023-07-03 73
serverC 2023-07-03 64

Task
1. Read the log above.
2. Detect the highest (maximum) CPU-usage number for every unique server name.
3. Produce a new plain-text file called:
      /home/user/analysis/peak_cpu_by_server.log
   (create the directory if it does not yet exist).

Exact output format that will be automatically verified
• One line per server, sorted **alphabetically by the server name** (ascending, case-sensitive).  
• Each line must be in the form:  
      SERVER_NAME|PEAK_CPU  
  where “|” is a literal vertical bar with no surrounding spaces.  
• Use Unix line endings (LF).  
• There must be **no heading line**, no trailing spaces, and the file must end with a single newline character.

Given the input log above, the correct /home/user/analysis/peak_cpu_by_server.log MUST contain exactly the three lines shown here:

serverA|35
serverB|79
serverC|64

No other output is required.

Hints (do not reveal exact commands)
• Combine standard text-processing tools to group by the first column and keep the maximum of the third column.
• Remember to sort alphabetically before writing the final file.
• The whole task can be done with 2-3 well-chosen commands.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

File: /home/user/data/usage.log  
Permissions: 644  
Content (9 lines, each terminated by LF):

serverA 2023-07-01 24
serverB 2023-07-01 67
serverC 2023-07-01 53
serverA 2023-07-02 21
serverB 2023-07-02 79
serverC 2023-07-02 48
serverA 2023-07-03 35
serverB 2023-07-03 73
serverC 2023-07-03 64

No /home/user/analysis directory exists initially.

Expected final state after the agent succeeds:

1. Directory /home/user/analysis created (permissions at least 755 so the agent can write).  
2. File /home/user/analysis/peak_cpu_by_server.log created with permissions 644 and exact content (including the terminating newline):

serverA|35
serverB|79
serverC|64

3. No stray files in /home/user/analysis (the test will fail if extra files are present).

Automated test steps:

a. Confirm that /home/user/analysis/peak_cpu_by_server.log exists.  
b. Compare its entire content byte-for-byte with the block above.  
c. Confirm that exactly three lines are present.  
d. Confirm directory cleanliness (only the single expected log file exists inside /home/user/analysis).  
e. Confirm that original /home/user/data/usage.log remains unchanged.

Any deviation will cause the test to fail.
name: task_000000_d01a1f48
